title: Aemetis Subsidiary Begins Biodiesel Deliveries Under $24M Allocation in India
content: Aemetis, Inc.'s subsidiary, Universal Biofuels, has commenced biodiesel deliveries in India under a $24 million allocation from the country's three state-owned Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs). The deal covers the supply of over 27 million liters of biodiesel for the period ending March 2026. This allocation supports India's National Policy on Biofuels, which targets increasing biodiesel blending from the current ~1% to 5%. Aemetis CEO Eric McAfee highlighted the need for rapid industry expansion to meet the governmentâ€™s 1.2 billion gallon annual target. Universal Biofuels, which operates an 80 million gallon-per-year plant in Kakinada, is also exploring an Initial Public Offering (IPO) in India to fund further expansion into biogas, ethanol, and Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF).
author: Analysts Account
